revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 子安神社 |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや はちおうしし}} [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 子安神社 |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔（たちばなうきょうしょうゆう）という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 ; 境内社 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神・[[大物主命]]） * 葦船社（祭神・[[ヒルコ|日留子命]]） * 末社五社 * [[厳島神社]] * 神水殿（祭神・[[木花開耶姫命]]） * 橘社（祭神・橘右京少輔（子安神社創建者）） * 祖霊社 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや はちおうしし}} [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 子安神社 |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔（たちばなうきょうしょうゆう）という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 ; 境内社 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神・[[大物主命]]） * 葦船社（祭神・[[ヒルコ|日留子命]]） * 末社五社 ** [[第六天神社]]（祭神・[[面足尊]]） ** [[御嶽神社]]（祭神・[[国常立尊]]） ** [[稲荷神社]]（祭神・[[保食神]]） ** [[白山神社]]（祭神・[[白山比咩神]]） ** 石神社（祭神・[[少彦名命]]） * [[厳島神社]] * 神水殿（祭神・[[木花開耶姫命]]） * 橘社（祭神・橘右京少輔（子安神社創建者）） * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == * 1月9日~10日 - 初こんぴら祭 * 2月3日 - [[節分祭]] * 3月 - 八王子きつね祭 * 4月 - 夜間参拝 * 6月30日 - [[大祓式]] * 7月 - 祇園祭 * 9月20日 - 例大祭（芸者衆の奉納演舞） * 9月[[秋分の日]] - [[泣き相撲]] * 10月 - 観月祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや はちおうしし}} [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 子安神社 |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔（たちばなうきょうしょうゆう）という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 ; 境内社 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神・[[大物主命]]） * 葦船社（祭神・[[ヒルコ|日留子命]]） * 末社五社 ** [[第六天神社]]（祭神・[[面足尊]]） ** [[御嶽神社]]（祭神・[[国常立尊]]） ** [[稲荷神社]]（祭神・[[保食神]]） ** [[白山神社]]（祭神・[[白山比咩神]]） ** 石神社（祭神・[[少彦名命]]） * [[厳島神社]] * 神水殿（祭神・[[木花開耶姫命]]） * 橘社（祭神・橘右京少輔（子安神社創建者）） * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == * 1月9日~10日 - 初こんぴら祭 * 2月3日 - [[節分祭]] * 3月 - 八王子きつね祭 * 4月 - 夜間参拝 * 6月30日 - [[大祓式]] * 7月 - 祇園祭 * 9月20日 - 例大祭（芸者衆の奉納演舞） * 9月[[秋分の日]] - [[泣き相撲]] * 10月 - 観月祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=子安村 子安明神社|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや はちおうしし}} [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 曖昧さ回避ページ[[欅]]へのリンクを解消、リンク先を[[ケヤキ]]に変更([[User:ネイ/DisamAssist|DisamAssist]]使用)
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 子安神社 |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔（たちばなうきょうしょうゆう）という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[ケヤキ|欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 ; 境内社 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神・[[大物主命]]） * 葦船社（祭神・[[ヒルコ|日留子命]]） * 末社五社 ** [[第六天神社]]（祭神・[[面足尊]]） ** [[御嶽神社]]（祭神・[[国常立尊]]） ** [[稲荷神社]]（祭神・[[保食神]]） ** [[白山神社]]（祭神・[[白山比咩神]]） ** 石神社（祭神・[[少彦名命]]） * [[厳島神社]] * 神水殿（祭神・[[木花開耶姫命]]） * 橘社（祭神・橘右京少輔（子安神社創建者）） * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == * 1月9日~10日 - 初こんぴら祭 * 2月3日 - [[節分祭]] * 3月 - 八王子きつね祭 * 4月 - 夜間参拝 * 6月30日 - [[大祓式]] * 7月 - 祇園祭 * 9月20日 - 例大祭（芸者衆の奉納演舞） * 9月[[秋分の日]] - [[泣き相撲]] * 10月 - 観月祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=子安村 子安明神社|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや はちおうしし}} [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 子安神社（八王子市） |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 JR八王子駅からは徒歩17分程度の場所に存在する。 　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　　（Google） [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔（たちばなうきょうしょうゆう）という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[ケヤキ|欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 ; 境内社 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神・[[大物主命]]） * 葦船社（祭神・[[ヒルコ|日留子命]]） * 末社五社 ** [[第六天神社]]（祭神・[[面足尊]]） ** [[御嶽神社]]（祭神・[[国常立尊]]） ** [[稲荷神社]]（祭神・[[保食神]]） ** [[白山神社]]（祭神・[[白山比咩神]]） ** 石神社（祭神・[[少彦名命]]） * [[厳島神社]] * 神水殿（祭神・[[木花開耶姫命]]） * 橘社（祭神・橘右京少輔（子安神社創建者）） * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == * 1月9日~10日 - 初こんぴら祭 * 2月3日 - [[節分祭]] * 3月 - 八王子きつね祭 * 4月 - 夜間参拝 * 6月30日 - [[大祓式]] * 7月 - 祇園祭 * 9月20日 - 例大祭（芸者衆の奉納演舞） * 9月[[秋分の日]] - [[泣き相撲]] * 10月 - 観月祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=子安村 子安明神社|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや はちおうしし}} [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔（たちばなうきょうしょうゆう）という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[ケヤキ|欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 ; 境内社 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神・[[大物主命]]） * 葦船社（祭神・[[ヒルコ|日留子命]]） * 末社五社 ** [[第六天神社]]（祭神・[[面足尊]]） ** [[御嶽神社]]（祭神・[[国常立尊]]） ** [[稲荷神社]]（祭神・[[保食神]]） ** [[白山神社]]（祭神・[[白山比咩神]]） ** 石神社（祭神・[[少彦名命]]） * [[厳島神社]] * 神水殿（祭神・[[木花開耶姫命]]） * 橘社（祭神・橘右京少輔（子安神社創建者）） * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == * 1月9日~10日 - 初こんぴら祭 * 2月3日 - [[節分祭]] * 3月 - 八王子きつね祭 * 4月 - 夜間参拝 * 6月30日 - [[大祓式]] * 7月 - 祇園祭 * 9月20日 - 例大祭（芸者衆の奉納演舞） * 9月[[秋分の日]] - [[泣き相撲]] * 10月 - 観月祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=子安村 子安明神社|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや}} [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot作業依頼#三つ葉葵から葵紋への改名に伴うリンク変更
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 子安神社 |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔（たちばなうきょうしょうゆう）という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[ケヤキ|欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[葵紋#三つ葉葵|三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 ; 境内社 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神・[[大物主命]]） * 葦船社（祭神・[[ヒルコ|日留子命]]） * 末社五社 ** [[第六天神社]]（祭神・[[面足尊]]） ** [[御嶽神社]]（祭神・[[国常立尊]]） ** [[稲荷神社]]（祭神・[[保食神]]） ** [[白山神社]]（祭神・[[白山比咩神]]） ** 石神社（祭神・[[少彦名命]]） * [[厳島神社]] * 神水殿（祭神・[[木花開耶姫命]]） * 橘社（祭神・橘右京少輔（子安神社創建者）） * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == * 1月9日~10日 - 初こんぴら祭 * 2月3日 - [[節分祭]] * 3月 - 八王子きつね祭 * 4月 - 夜間参拝 * 6月30日 - [[大祓式]] * 7月 - 祇園祭 * 9月20日 - 例大祭（芸者衆の奉納演舞） * 9月[[秋分の日]] - [[泣き相撲]] * 10月 - 観月祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=子安村 子安明神社|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや}} [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 子安神社 |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔（たちばなうきょうしょうゆう）という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[ケヤキ|欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[葵紋#三つ葉葵|三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 ; 境内社 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神・[[大物主命]]） * 葦船社（祭神・[[ヒルコ|日留子命]]） * 末社五社 ** [[第六天神社]]（祭神・[[面足尊]]） ** [[御嶽神社]]（祭神・[[国常立尊]]） ** [[稲荷神社]]（祭神・[[保食神]]） ** [[白山神社]]（祭神・[[白山比咩神]]） ** 石神社（祭神・[[少彦名命]]） * [[厳島神社]] * 神水殿（祭神・[[木花開耶姫命]]） * 橘社（祭神・橘右京少輔（子安神社創建者）） * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == * 1月9日~10日 - 初こんぴら祭 * 2月3日 - [[節分祭]] * 3月 - 八王子きつね祭 * 4月 - 夜間参拝 * 6月30日 - [[大祓式]] * 7月 - 祇園祭 * 9月20日 - 例大祭（芸者衆の奉納演舞） * 9月[[秋分の日]] - [[泣き相撲]] * 10月 - 観月祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=子安村 子安明神社|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや}} [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

revision.text:
'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to deities associated with [[safe childbirth]] and [[child-rearing]].
Shrines are generally divided into those enshrining [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], who is also the deity of [[Mount Fuji]] (and the main deity of [[Sengen Shrine]]), and those enshrining [[Kushinadahime]], the wife of [[Susanoo]]. Both deities are genealogically related, with the former being the child and the latter being the grandchild of [[Ōyamatsumi]].
== Kanto Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Mito)|ja|子安神社 (水戸市)}} - Located in Chidami, Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kasumigaura)|ja|子安神社 (かすみがうら市)}} - Located in Higashinoji, Kasumigaura City, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Tone)|ja|子安神社 (利根町)}} - Located in Obusa, Tone Town, Kita-Soma District, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|ja|櫻井子安神社}} - Located in Sakurai, Asahi City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Sosa)|ja|子安神社 (匝瑳市)}} - Located in Yokoshiba, Sosa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hanamigawa)|ja|子安神社 (千葉市花見川区)}} - Located in Hatamachi, Hanamigawa Ward, Chiba City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|ja|子安神社 (市川市)}} - Located in Kashiwaimachi, Ichikawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|ja|子安神社 (習志野市)}} - Located in Fujisaki, Narashino City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Matsudo)|ja|子安神社 (松戸市)}} - Located in Yagasaki, Matsudo City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine|ja|子安八幡神社}} - Located in Nakaiikegami and Kitakojiya, Ota Ward, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|ja|子安神社 (八王子市)}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|ja|子安神社 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|ja|子安宮澤神社}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|ja|子安八幡神社 (甲府市)}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|ja|子安神社 (河津町)}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|ja|新橋浅間神社}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|子安神社 (富士宮市)}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|ja|子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|子安神社 (浜松市東区)}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|ja|子安神社 (岐阜市)}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|ja|子安神社 (大垣市)}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|ja|子安神社 (大府市)}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|ja|子安神社 (加賀市)}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|ja|子安神社 (金沢市)}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社 (伊勢市)}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|ja|子安神社 (紀宝町)}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|ja|子安神社 (岡山市)}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|子守神社}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|ja|子育明神}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a type of [[Shinto shrine|Shi]]Nakaiikegami and Kitakojiya, Ota Ward, Tokyo.

{{Shrine |名称 = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} |社格 = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (Deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (Deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (Deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (Deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - {{Shrine |名称 = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} |社格 = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (Deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (Deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (Deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (Deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - {{Shrine |名称 = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} |社格 = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main Deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (Deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (Deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (Deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - {{Shrine |名称 = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} |社格 = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - {{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} |社格 = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:新橋浅間神社]] - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:吉備津彦神社]]. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子守神社]] * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子育明神]] * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安地蔵]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安地蔵堂]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子安地蔵寺]] * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子守地蔵]] * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:子育地蔵]] * {{ill|Suitengū|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[ja:水天宮]] {{Shinto-stub]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrines with identical names]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[ja:子安神社]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[ja:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:qq]]

Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - {{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)-qq]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]]. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Komori According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ** {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ** {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ** {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ** {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ** Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 -
