{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - {{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - {{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子守神社]] * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子育明神]] * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] * {{ill|Suitengū|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:水天宮]] {{Shinto-stub]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[ja:子安神社]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[Category:getting the categories and imported pages jawiki linked]][[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子守神社]] * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子育明神]] * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] * {{ill|Suitengū|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:水天宮]] {{Shinto-stub]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[ja:子安神社]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子守神社]] * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子育明神]] * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] * {{ill|Suitengū|en}}[[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[ja:水天宮]] {{Shinto-stub]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[ja:子安神社]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:pages goodbye $1]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子守神社]] * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子育明神]] * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] * {{ill|Suitengū|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:水天宮]] {{Shinto-stub]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[ja:子安神社]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:皇大神宮]]. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]].== See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子守神社]] * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子育明神]] * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] * {{ill|Suitengū|en}}[[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[ja:水天宮]] {{Shinto-stub]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]][[ja:子安神社]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]][[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943年}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - {{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759年}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943rd year }} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897年}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945年}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] -
