Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories|[[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]]|12=draft}} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅|12=draft}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=draft}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=draft}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=draft}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|12=draft}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿|12=draft}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|12=draft}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|12=draft}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|12=draft}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=draft}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=draft}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲|12=draft}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en|12=draft}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories|[[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]]|12=draft}} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]]

A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories|[[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] |12=draft}} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|天照大神|" to "|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅|12=draft}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=draft}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=draft}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=draft}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|12=draft}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿|12=draft}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|12=draft}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|12=draft}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|12=draft}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=draft}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=draft}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲|12=draft}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en|12=draft}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories|[[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] |12=draft}} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]]

A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories| |12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅|12=draft}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=draft}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=draft}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=draft}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|12=draft}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿|12=draft}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|12=draft}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|12=draft}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|12=draft}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=draft}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=draft}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲|12=draft}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories| |12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]

Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|12=draft|12=draft}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|12=draft|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories| |12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759th year}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759th year}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943rd year}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}} . * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]]

{{神社 |名称 = 子安神社 |画像 = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[木花開耶姫命]] |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月20日[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ 子安神社] - 東京都神社庁 |神事 = }} '''子安神社'''（こやすじんじゃ）とは、[[東京都]][[八王子市]]明神町にある神社。八王子最古の神社とされる[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト。 == 概要 == [[京王八王子駅]]から北西に徒歩1分程度の距離に所在している。 [[759年]]（[[天平宝字]]3年）に橘右京少輔（たちばなうきょうしょうゆう）という人物が、当時の天皇（[[淳仁天皇]]）の皇后の安産祈願のために創建したと『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』に記されている[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html 由緒] - 子安神社{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}。その由緒もあり、子安神社は今日まで安産の神社として多くの崇敬を集めている。 また古来より武士からの尊崇も篤く、[[源義家]]が奥州討伐の際には戦勝を祈念して[[ケヤキ|欅]]（[[けやき]]）を船形に植樹しており、近隣の地名「船森」として名残りを留めている。 近世では[[徳川家光]]以降代々の将軍家から[[朱印]]を受けており、その縁で神社の御紋は徳川家の家紋である[[葵紋#三つ葉葵|三つ葉葵]]が採用されている。 [[1872年]]（明治5年）に村社に列せられ、[[1943年]]（昭和18年）には郷社に昇格した。 [[1897年]]（明治30年）4月22日に発生した[[八王子大火]]と、[[第二次世界大戦]]中の[[1945年]]（昭和20年）8月2日における[[八王子空襲]]によって、2度社殿を全焼した。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[木花開耶姫命]] ; その他 * [[大山咋命]] * [[素戔鳴尊]] * [[天照大神]] * [[奇稲田姫命]] == 境内 == * [[神楽殿]] * 御神池 ; 境内社 * [[金刀比羅神社]]（祭神・[[大物主命]]） * 葦船社（祭神・[[ヒルコ|日留子命]]） * 末社五社 ** [[第六天神社]]（祭神・[[面足尊]]） ** [[御嶽神社]]（祭神・[[国常立尊]]） ** [[稲荷神社]]（祭神・[[保食神]]） ** [[白山神社]]（祭神・[[白山比咩神]]） ** 石神社（祭神・[[少彦名命]]） * [[厳島神社]] * 神水殿（祭神・[[木花開耶姫命]]） * 橘社（祭神・橘右京少輔（子安神社創建者）） * 祖霊社 == 祭事 == * 1月9日~10日 - 初こんぴら祭 * 2月3日 - [[節分祭]] * 3月 - 八王子きつね祭 * 4月 - 夜間参拝 * 6月30日 - [[大祓式]] * 7月 - 祇園祭 * 9月20日 - 例大祭（芸者衆の奉納演舞） * 9月[[秋分の日]] - [[泣き相撲]] * 10月 - 観月祭 * 12月31日 - 大祓式 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=子安村 子安明神社|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 子安村}}}} == 外部リンク == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ 子安神社] - 公式サイト {{DEFAULTSORT:こやすしんしや}} [[Category:東京都の神社]] [[Category:東京都の旧郷社]] [[Category:八王子市の建築物]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[子安神社 (八王子市)]] to [[Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

拝殿

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759th year}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-Hachiōji Station|ja|京王八王子駅}}. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|ja|759th year}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943rd year}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神|1=Ukemochi}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en}} . * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] {{translated page|ja|子安神社 (八王子市)|version=101429652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}} {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|12=draft}} |創建 = {{ill|759|ja|759th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|ja|八王子市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, in Myojin-cho. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|ja|ケヤキ|12=draft}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|ja|徳川家光|12=draft}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=draft}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|12=draft}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|ja|1943rd year|12=draft}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=draft}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|ja|1897th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=draft}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|ja|1945th year|12=draft}} during {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔鳴尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|12=draft}} == Shrine Grounds == * {{ill|Kaguraden|ja|神楽殿|12=draft}} (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|12=draft}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Kunitokotachi|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|保食神|1=Ukemochi|12=draft}}) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=draft}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|12=draft}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|12=draft}} * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=draft}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=draft}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo|ja|泣き相撲|12=draft}} (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=draft}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en|12=draft}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|en|12=draft}} . * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|12=draft}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{draft categories||12=draft}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|子安神社 (八王子市)|version=101429652|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachiōji]] [[Category:Completed disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to child-rearing deities]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine]] [[Category:Childbirth]] [[Category:Parenting]] [[Category:Koyasu_Shrine forks]] [[Category:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] }} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Kami|qq=}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=}} (deity: [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]]) ''' [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (deity: [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirayama-hime|qq=}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|qq=}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * {{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == {{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Kami|qq=}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=}} (deity: [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]]) ''' [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (deity: [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirayama-hime|qq=}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}) * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|qq=}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}} == External Links == * [https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|qq=}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|qq=}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture. == Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasumiya Shrine|qq=}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|qq=}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|qq=}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|qq=}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu
