(Gifu)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|qq=}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|qq=}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|qq=}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|qq=}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture. == Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} . * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|qq=}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture. == Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|qq=}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu Shrine|qq=}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Myōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō Hall|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|qq=}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengū|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{translated page|ja|子安神社 (八王子市)|version=101429652|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories||qq=draft}} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:八王子市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War {{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu Shrine|qq=}} . == See Also == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Myōjin|qq=}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō Hall|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō Temple|qq=}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komori Jizō|qq=}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosodate Jizō|qq=}} * {{ill|Suitengū|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suitengū|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub]] {{translated page|ja|子安神社 (八王子市)|version=101429652|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories||qq=draft}} [[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢原市)]] [[ja:子安宮澤神社]] [[ja:子安八幡神社 (甲府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (河津町)]] [[ja:新橋浅間神社]] [[ja:子安神社 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (浜松市東区)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岐阜市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大垣市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (大府市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (加賀市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (金沢市)]] [[ja:子安神社 (伊勢市)]] [[ja:皇大神宮]] [[ja:子安神社 (紀宝町)]] [[ja:子安神社 (岡山市)]] [[ja:吉備津彦神社]] [[ja:子守神社]] [[ja:子育明神]] [[ja:子安地蔵]] [[ja:子安地蔵堂]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子守地蔵]] [[ja:子育地蔵]] [[ja:水天宮]] [[ja:子安神社]] [[sn:oyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[sn:Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]]

{{Shrine |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond ; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=}} (deity: [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]]) ''' [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (deity: [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} (deity: Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * Shin Suiden (deity: コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine) == Festivals == * January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q116768256]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = [[File:Koyasu jinja.jpg|260px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E}}}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association |神事 = }} '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website == Overview == The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]]. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day. The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森). During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=}}. In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine). The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]]. == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu redirect}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to '''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Enshrined Deities ==

; Main deity
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

; Additional Deities
* {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}}
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
* {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}

== Shrine Grounds ==

* [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (Sacred Dance Hall)
* Sacred Pond

; Auxiliary Shrines
* {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}})
* Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
* Five Subordinate Shrines:
''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11152535}})
''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|qq=|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} (deity: [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]])
''' [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (deity: [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]])
''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神|1=Kukurihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619891}})
''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}})
* [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]
* Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}})
* Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine)
* Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine)

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|qq=|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|qq=|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

|創建   = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}}
|name   = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社)
|image   = File:Koyasu jinja.jpg
|location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3
|位置   = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}}
|deity   = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}}
|type   = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建   = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|festival   = September 20<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association</ref>
|神事   = 
|prefecture   = {{ill|Hachioji|WD=Q208863}}
|coordinates   = {{Coord|35.659083|N|139.341306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Enshrined Deities ==

; Main deity
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

; Additional Deities
* {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}}
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
* {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}

== Shrine Grounds ==

* [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (Sacred Dance Hall)
* Sacred Pond

; Auxiliary Shrines
* {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}})
* Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
* Five Subordinate Shrines:
''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11152535}})
''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} (deity: [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]])
''' [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (deity: [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]])
''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神|1=Kukurihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619891}})
''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}})
* [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]
* Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}})
* Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine)
* Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine)

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)
