'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].
According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.
The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>
In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>
The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116768256]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = File:Koyasu jinja.jpg |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} |type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association</ref> |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hachioji|WD=Q208863}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.659083|N|139.341306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].
According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.
The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>
During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>
In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>
The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q116768256}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hachiōji]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Hachioji]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116768256]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = File:Koyasu jinja.jpg |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} |type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association</ref> |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hachioji|WD=Q208863}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.659083|N|139.341306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].
According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.
The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>
During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>
In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>
The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}
== Shrine Grounds ==
* [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond
; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11152535}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} (deity: [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]]) ''' [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (deity: [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神|1=Kukurihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619891}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}) * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine)
== Festivals ==
* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:子安神社 (八王子市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116768256]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767}} |name = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社) |image = File:Koyasu jinja.jpg |location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]] Myojin-cho 4-10-3 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862}} |type = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}} |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |festival = September 20<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/minamitama/hachiouji/3184/ Koyasu Shrine] - Tokyo Shrine Association</ref> |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Hachioji|WD=Q208863}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.659083|N|139.341306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hachiōji|Hachiōji]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>
== Overview ==
The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of [[:en:Keiō-hachiōji Station|Keiō-Hachiōji Station]].
According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in [[:en:759|759]] ([[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Emperor Junnin]].<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.
The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]], during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ([[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova serrata]]) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>
During the early modern period, starting with [[:en:Tokugawa Iemitsu|Tokugawa Iemitsu]], successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>
In [[:en:1872|1872]] (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in [[:en:1943|1943]] (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>
The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, [[:en:1897|1897]] (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, [[:en:1945|1945]] during [[:en:World War II|World War II]].<ref name="yuisho"/>
== Enshrined Deities ==
; Main deity * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}
== Shrine Grounds ==
* [[:en:Kagura-den|Kaguraden]] (Sacred Dance Hall) * Sacred Pond
; Auxiliary Shrines * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}) * Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) * Five Subordinate Shrines: ''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11152535}}) ''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} (deity: [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]]) ''' [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] (deity: [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi]]) ''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神|1=Kukurihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619891}}) ''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}) * [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] * Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}) * Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine) * Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine)
== Festivals ==
* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival * February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}} * March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival * April - Night Worship Event * June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony) * July - Gion Festival * September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances) * September (Autumn Equinox) - [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Naki Sumo]] (Crying Sumo Festival) * October - Moon Viewing Festival * December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Sources ==
* {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}|volume=巻ノ102上多磨郡ノ14上|chapter=Koyasu Village Koyasu Myojin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763990/83}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}}}

== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasumiya Shrine|qq=}}
- Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|qq=}}
- Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|qq=}}
- Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|qq=}}
- Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|qq=}}
- Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|qq=}}
- Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=}}
- Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|qq=}}
- Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|qq=}}
- Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|qq=}}
- Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|qq=}}
- Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|qq=}}
- Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}
- Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|qq=}}
- Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|qq=}}
- Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsu Shrine|qq=}}

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Enshrined Deities ==

; Main deity
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

; Additional Deities
* {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}}

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Naki Sumo}} (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

|name   = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社)

|location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}} {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3

|本殿   =

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Enshrined Deities ==

; Main deity
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

; Additional Deities
* {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}}
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}

== Shrine Grounds ==

* {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden}} (Sacred Dance Hall)
* Sacred Pond

; Auxiliary Shrines
* {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}})
* Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
* Five Subordinate Shrines:
''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11152535}})
''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} (deity: {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}})
''' {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} (deity: {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi}})
''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神|1=Kukurihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619891}})
''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}})
* {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}}
* Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}})
* Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine)
* Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine)

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Naki Sumo}} (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Naki Sumo}} (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Naki Sumo}} (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

|name   = Koyasu Shrine (子安神社)
|image   = File:Koyasu jinja.jpg
|location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji|1=Hachiōji}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3
|位置   = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}}

|type   = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建   = {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759|1=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 =

|神事   = 
|prefecture   = {{ill|Hachioji|WD=Q208863|1=Hachiōji}}

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station|1=Keiō-hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759|1=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata|1=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Enshrined Deities ==

; Main deity
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

; Additional Deities
* {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}}
* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|奇稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}

== Shrine Grounds ==

* {{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura-den}} (Sacred Dance Hall)
* Sacred Pond

; Auxiliary Shrines
* {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (deity: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}})
* Ashibune Shrine (deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|日留子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})
* Five Subordinate Shrines:
''' {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Omotaru-no-Kami|ja|面足尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omotaru-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモダル・アヤカシコネ|1=Omodaru and Ayakashikone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11152535}})
''' {{ill|Ontake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ontake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488964}} (deity: {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}})
''' {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine|1=Inari shrine}} (deity: {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}})
''' {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Shirayama-hime|ja|白山比咩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神|1=Kukurihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619891}})
''' Ishi Shrine (deity: {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}})
* {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}}
* Shin Suiden (deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}})
* Tachibana Shrine (deity: Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu, the founder of Koyasu Shrine)
* Sorei Shrine (Ancestor Worship Shrine)

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Naki Sumo|1=Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival}} (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station|1=Keiō-hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759|1=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata|1=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Naki Sumo|1=Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival}} (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station|1=Keiō-hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759|1=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata|1=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Enshrined Deities ==

; Main deity
* {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}

; Additional Deities
* {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}}

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}}

* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Naki Sumo|1=Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival}} (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)

|location = {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}} {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji|1=Hachiōji}} Myojin-cho 4-10-3
|位置   = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|32.7|N|139|20|28.7|E|display=inline,title}}
|deity   = {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|WD=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
|type   = Former {{ill|Gōsha (Shinto)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
|創建   = {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759|1=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3){{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}
|本殿   =

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|WD=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|WD=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station|1=Keiō-hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|WD=Q30532|lt=759|1=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|WD=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|WD=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata|1=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|WD=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|WD=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|WD=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|qid=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|qid=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station|1=Keiō-hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|qid=Q30532|lt=759|1=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|qid=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|qid=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata|1=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|qid=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|qid=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|qid=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|qid=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|qid=Q1461738|lt=Naki Sumo|1=Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival}} (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)
