'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hachiōji|en|Hachiōji|qid=Q208863|lt=Hachiōji|1=Hachiōji}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, in Myojin-cho. It is considered the oldest shrine in Hachiōji.<ref>[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/ Koyasu Shrine] - Official Website</ref>

== Overview ==

The shrine is located about a one-minute walk northwest of {{ill|Keiō-hachiōji Station|en|Keiō-hachiōji Station|qid=Q906143|lt=Keiō-Hachiōji Station|1=Keiō-hachiōji Station}}.

According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}}'', the shrine was founded in {{ill|759|en|759|qid=Q30532|lt=759|1=759}} ({{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 3) by a man named Tachibana Ukyo-no-Shōyu (橘右京少輔) to pray for the safe childbirth of the empress of the reigning emperor, {{ill|Emperor Junnin|en|Emperor Junnin|qid=Q329713|lt=Emperor Junnin|1=Emperor Junnin}}.<ref name="jinjacho"/><ref name="yuisho">[https://koyasujinja.or.jp/yuisyo.html History] - Koyasu Shrine</ref>{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Koyasu Village}}. Because of this history, Koyasu Shrine has been revered as a place of prayer for safe childbirth up to the present day.

The shrine has also historically received devotion from warriors. It is said that {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}}, during his campaign against the Ōshū region, planted a keyaki ({{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|qid=Q920344|lt=Zelkova serrata|1=Zelkova serrata}}) tree in the shape of a ship as a votive offering, and this is said to be the origin of the nearby place name "Funamori" (船森).<ref name="yuisho"/>

During the early modern period, starting with {{ill|Tokugawa Iemitsu|en|Tokugawa Iemitsu|qid=Q314481|lt=Tokugawa Iemitsu|1=Tokugawa Iemitsu}}, successive shoguns granted the shrine {{ill|Shuin|ja|朱印|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin|qq=}}, a mark of official recognition. Because of this connection, the shrine's crest is the same as that of the Tokugawa family, the {{ill|Aoi-mon|ja|葵紋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoi-mon|1=Aoi mon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353976}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

In {{ill|1872|en|1872|qid=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), the shrine was Ranked as a ''mura-sha'' (village shrine), and in {{ill|1943|en|1943|qid=Q18623|lt=1943|1=1943}} (Shōwa 18), it was elevated to the status of ''gōsha'' (district shrine).<ref name="yuisho"/>

The shrine was completely destroyed twice, first in the {{ill|Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja|八王子大火|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Fire of Hachiōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on April 22, {{ill|1897|en|1897|qid=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} (Meiji 30), and again in the {{ill|Hachiōji Air Raid|ja|八王子空襲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiōji Air Raid|1=Bombing of Hachiōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391620}} on August 2, {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|qid=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}.<ref name="yuisho"/>

== Festivals ==

* January 9-10 - Hatsukonpira Festival
* February 3 - {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q746798}}
* March - Hachiōji Kitsune Festival
* April - Night Worship Event
* June 30 - {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōharae|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓}} (Great Purification Ceremony)
* July - Gion Festival
* September 20 - Annual Grand Festival (Featuring geisha performances)
* September (Autumn Equinox) - {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|qid=Q1461738|lt=Naki Sumo|1=Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival}} (Crying Sumo Festival)
* October - Moon Viewing Festival
* December 31 - Ōharae (Great Purification Ceremony)
